source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage Crane/Lifting Device

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - Mud Pit Discharge
actions_taken: Changed operational procedures for moving fluids between mud tanks
Planned installation of additional valve downstream
Added procedures for reporting incidents to BSEE
event.summary: On 11 February 2014, an estimated 144 barrels of Synthetic Base Mud was discharged into the Gulf of Mexico from the #3 Active Pit dump valve during a mud pit cleaning and transfer operation. The incident resulted from failure to follow the Job Safety Analysis (JSA), improper Lock Out/Tag Out procedures, and inadequate sampling prior to discharge. The #3 Dump Valve was left in the open position and not properly identified during the operation.
recommendations: Installation of additional isolation valve in piping layout to provide barrier against accidental discharge
Enhanced procedures for opening and closing overboard discharge valves
Improved Lock Out/Tag Out execution and verification
Proper sampling from correct pit location before discharge authorization

hazards.name: Mud pit cleaning and transfer operation

threats.name: Dump valve left in open position
Lack of isolation valve in piping design

consequences.name: Unauthorized discharge of mud to Gulf of Mexico

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for mud transfer operation
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure proper procedures are followed

supporting_text: The JSA dated February 12, 2014, titled Transferring mud from reserve pit to active pits utilized for the mud transferring operation during the time of incident occurring on February 11, 2014, does not make any mention for a procedure assuring the proper alignment of all dump valves
Not all persons responsible for opening and closing the mud pit valves had signed the JSA for the mud transferring operation
The JSA was not properly completed or executed due to incorrect date when the document was generated

controls.name: Lock Out/Tag Out (LO/TO) procedures for dump valve identification
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure valves are properly identified and secured

supporting_text: all personnel performing the job tasks during the time of incident failed to review the proper Lock Out/ Tag Out safety steps to be taken in order to properly identify the #3 Dump Valve was still in the open position
documented in the H&P LO/TO/TRY Record Sheet

controls.name: Static Sheen Test sampling from correct pit location
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to verify fluid composition before discharge

supporting_text: The Lessee failed to properly gather a sample for the Static Sheen Test at the correct sample location prior to releasing pit fluids to gulf waters during the pit cleaning operation
Samples were gathered from the #4 Auxiliary Tank and the Trip Tank, but not the #3 Active Pit
The Dump Valve on the #3 Active Pit was utilized to discharge the pit fluids into the gulf waters

controls.name: Isolation valve in piping layout design
controls.barrier_role: Engineering barrier to prevent accidental discharge

supporting_text: Piping layout design did have an isolation valve which would have provided another barrier against accidental discharge of mud into the Gulf of Mexico

Shell Offshore INC. has changed their operational procedures taken by H&P personnel for moving fluids between the mud tanks along with the planned installation of an additional valve downstream to eliminate the possibility of the issue happening again

controls.name: Personnel sign-off on JSA
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure personnel acknowledge procedures

supporting_text: Not all persons responsible for opening and closing the mud pit valves had signed the JSA for the mud transferring operation

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
